ideas.title: Cellular Circuit Mapping of Memory Deficits via Microfluidic Co-culture
ideas.description: This idea applies the solution from tissue engineering and organ-on-a-chip technology to the original problem of scaling down memory models. The analogous field solved the problem of studying complex organ functions by building controlled, simplified cellular microenvironments in microfluidic devices. We adapt this by creating a 'memory circuit-on-a-chip' model. A microfluidic co-culture system will compartmentalize and connect key neuronal cell types (e.g., from hippocampus and entorhinal cortex) derived from model rodents with known NOR performance. This allows for high-resolution, causal investigation of synaptic plasticity and network dynamics within a defined, essential memory circuit in vitro. The in vitro functional readouts from this simplified system can then be directly correlated with the animal's behavioral NOR deficits, providing a more precise and mechanistically tractable model than whole brain slices.

ideas.title: Apply Activity-Based Protein Profiling (ABPP) with a ketamine-derived probe to map its off-target interactome
ideas.description: This idea adapts the solution of Activity-Based Protein Profiling (ABPP) from the field of enzymology and covalent drug discovery to the problem of mapping ketamine's off-targets. In ABPP, a small molecule is converted into a chemical probe with a warhead and a tag to covalently capture and identify its binding proteins. We propose to synthesize a ketamine-based ABPP probe featuring a photo-crosslinker (e.g., diazirine) and an affinity handle (e.g., biotin). This probe will be used in chemoproteomic experiments to irreversibly crosslink to ketamine's binding partners in relevant biological systems upon UV irradiation. The biotin tag will then allow for streptavidin-based enrichment of probe-bound proteins, which will be digested and identified by liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS). This adapted approach will generate an unbiased, proteome-wide profile of ketamine's direct protein interactors, revealing novel off-target sites that may contribute to its complex pharmacological and side-effect profiles.

ideas.title: Apply ecological population dynamics models to NOR memory consolidation
ideas.description: This idea adapts solution methods from theoretical ecology to the problem of quantifying NOR performance. In ecology, differential equation models (e.g., Lotka-Volterra) are used to quantify population interactions, stability, and resilience to environmental perturbations. These models treat species populations as dynamic systems with parameters for intrinsic growth, competition coefficients, and environmental carrying capacity, enabling prediction of system collapse or recovery after a disturbance. We adapt this approach by conceptualizing memory traces for novel and familiar objects in the NOR task as interacting 'populations' competing for limited neural representation resources. A computational model would be developed with parameters analogous to ecological ones: encoding strength (growth rate), interference between memories (competition coefficient), and consolidation capacity (carrying capacity). By fitting this model to NOR behavioral data (exploration times across trials), we can quantify the underlying memory system's 'stability' and 'resilience,' moving beyond simple discrimination ratios to predict the severity of memory deficits from specific task parameters and interventions, offering a more mechanistic account of episodic memory dysfunction.

ideas.title: Longitudinal Cross-Modal Object Recognition Battery with Novelty Preservation
ideas.description: This redesigned approach tracks cognitive function from adolescence to adulthood using a battery of object recognition tests that vary by sensory modality and object set at each time point, preventing habituation. Each session tests recognition memory within a novel sensory context (e.g., visual objects, textured objects, scented objects) to maintain the essential 'novelty' component. The protocol includes controls for general exploration drive and sensory acuity at each age. This allows for the isolation of true declarative memory trajectories from the confounding effects of repeated testing inherent in the standard longitudinal NOR design.

ideas.title: Adapting Microfluidic Cell Culture Arrays to Scale the Novel Object Recognition Test
ideas.description: This idea adapts the solution from high-throughput cell biology—specifically, microfluidic cell culture arrays—to solve the scaling problem of the Novel Object Recognition (NOR) test. In cell biology, microfluidic arrays enabled the miniaturization and parallelization of complex assays by using microfabricated chambers with automated fluid and environmental control. Adapted to behavioral neuroscience, this would involve creating a 'behavior-on-a-chip' system: a multi-chamber microfluidic device where individual rodents sequentially experience automated NOR trials in isolated mini-arenas. Each chamber would have controlled object presentation (likely via retractable or interchangeable object ports), integrated video tracking for automated discrimination scoring, and environmental control. This system would allow dozens to hundreds of parallel, miniaturized NOR trials to run autonomously, dramatically increasing throughput for screening cognitive-enhancing compounds from large libraries while using far less space and reducing inter-trial variability through automation.

papers.title: Association study of the vesicular monoamine transporter gene SLC18A2 with tardive dyskinesia.
papers.abstract: Tardive dyskinesia (TD) is an involuntary movement disorder that can occur in up to 25% of patients receiving long-term first-generation antipsychotic treatment.
